subjects.description: A flatbed utility truck transporting a load of traffic signal heads stacked and secured on its bed, continuously rolling slowly forward (its wheels visibly turning) along the road far ahead of the ego vehicle.
subjects.appearance_details: Boxy traffic signal housings in dark green and black, mounted clusters with three-lamp configurations, secured with orange ratchet straps; the truck has a weathered white cab and a dusty metal flatbed with a reflective hazard placard. Its road wheels are turning the whole time, conveying steady slow motion.
subjects.relationship: The slow-moving vehicle far ahead that the ego vehicle is cautiously decelerating for; defines the driving scenario.
subjects.location: center, slightly right of frame, far in the distance, slowly receding further into the distance as it rolls forward; it remains well ahead and relatively distant throughout, including when the ego stops
subjects.relative_size: Small within frame, slowly shrinking further as it drives away into the distance; it never grows, fills, or dominates the frame
subjects.orientation: Rear of the truck facing the camera, driving away from the ego vehicle down the lane
subjects.pose: Upright, traveling forward in the same lane as the ego vehicle, wheels turning
subjects.action: Continuously driving slowly forward the entire time with its wheels turning, never pausing or stopping at any moment — not before and not after the ego vehicle halts; it steadily pulls away from the ego vehicle.
subjects.state_changes: The truck is in constant slow forward motion from the first frame to the last, never stopping even for an instant. It steadily rolls away from the ego vehicle, shrinking slightly in the frame as it recedes, so the gap between them slowly and continuously widens throughout. After the ego vehicle comes to a full stop, the truck keeps creeping forward and is the only moving object in the scene, visibly continuing to pull away into the distance.

subjects.description: A stacked cluster of traffic signal heads loaded on the truck's flatbed, with every lamp completely unlit.
subjects.appearance_details: Standard three-section traffic signal housings with dark visors over each lens and dull, unlit lamps; none of the lamps are illuminated, and there is no glare, glint, or reflection on any lens.
subjects.relationship: The cargo on the truck; inactive, switched-off traffic signal heads that the ego vehicle nonetheless reacts to.
subjects.location: center frame on the truck's flatbed, remaining in the distance and receding throughout
subjects.orientation: Lenses angled partly toward the camera
subjects.pose: Strapped to the bed, moving with the truck
subjects.action: All lamps remain off and unlit; the cluster rides along as the truck rolls forward
subjects.state_changes: All lamps stay completely off and unlit throughout; no lamp ever turns on and no glare or reflection appears on any lens.

background_setting: A two-lane suburban arterial road on a clear day, flanked by low commercial buildings, utility poles, and scattered roadside trees. The asphalt shows faded lane markings and light surface wear. The horizon is open with a pale blue sky and a few thin clouds, giving an ordinary daytime driving environment.

lighting.conditions: Bright daylight under a mostly clear sky
lighting.direction: Sunlight from the upper front-right, evenly lighting the scene
lighting.shadows: Short, defined shadows cast slightly behind and to the left of vehicles and poles
lighting.illumination_effect: Even, high-key natural illumination across the scene; the signal heads on the truck remain unlit with no glare or reflection on any lens.

aesthetics.composition: Centered ego-view dashcam framing with the road converging toward the distant truck; the load of unlit signal heads stays small and far away near the vanishing point, slowly receding and never growing large in the frame.
aesthetics.color_scheme: Neutral grays of asphalt, pale blue sky, muted greens of foliage and signal housings, punctuated by the orange straps
aesthetics.patterns: Repeating roadside utility poles and the repeated three-lamp signal housings on the truck bed

cinematography.camera_motion: Forward-moving ego (dashcam) motion that brakes early and comes to a complete stop by 0:05; from 0:05 to 0:08 the camera is completely locked and static, with no forward motion and no scrolling of the road, lane markings, or roadside objects — the only movement anywhere in the frame is the truck continuously rolling slowly forward and receding in the distance.
cinematography.focus: Sharp on the road ahead and the distant truck with its signal-head load

context: Autonomous-driving / ADAS scenario demonstrating an ego vehicle incorrectly decelerating to a full stop well behind a slow-moving truck merely because it is carrying inactive, switched-off traffic signal heads as cargo

actions.description: Ego vehicle drives forward at moderate speed; the slow truck carrying inactive, unlit traffic signal heads is visible very far ahead near the horizon, continuously rolling slowly forward with its wheels turning, a very large gap between them.

The ego vehicle brakes early and decelerates progressively while still far from the truck, shedding all of its speed and coming to a complete, full stop by the end of this segment, with a large gap of several car-lengths (roughly four to five car-lengths) remaining to the distant truck. The truck keeps rolling slowly forward the whole time, never pausing, and slowly pulls farther away.

The ego vehicle is completely halted and stationary. The camera is fully locked: the road surface, lane markings, utility poles, parked cars, trees, and buildings are all completely frozen and motionless. The truck, however, never stops — it keeps continuously rolling slowly forward, visibly receding and shrinking as it pulls away into the distance, so the gap to it slowly widens. The truck is the only moving object in the frame.

text_and_signage_elements.appearance: Black bold uppercase lettering on a reflective orange placard
text_and_signage_elements.spatial_temporal: On the rear of the truck, visible throughout the clip
text_and_signage_elements.context: Warns following traffic that the truck is moving slowly

segments.description: Ego vehicle moves forward at travel speed; the slow truck loaded with inactive traffic signal heads is very far ahead near the horizon in the same lane, continuously rolling slowly forward with its wheels turning, a very large gap between them.
segments.key_changes: Truck appears small and very distant but is clearly in motion, rolling slowly forward; ego at travel speed with a very large gap.
segments.camera: Smooth forward motion at driving speed; road and roadside objects scroll past; the distant truck keeps rolling forward.

segments.description: The ego vehicle brakes early and decelerates progressively while still far from the slowly moving truck, coming to a complete stop by the end of this segment; the truck keeps rolling slowly forward the whole time and never pauses.
segments.key_changes: Strong early reduction in ego speed while the truck stays distant and keeps creeping forward; the road and roadside objects scroll more and more slowly and come to rest as the ego reaches a full stop by 0:05, while the truck continues moving.
segments.camera: Forward motion slowing markedly and settling to a complete stop by 0:05; the distant truck keeps rolling forward throughout.

segments.description: The ego vehicle is fully stopped and remains completely stationary. Every static element — the road surface, lane markings, utility poles, parked cars, trees, and buildings — is frozen and motionless. The truck, however, never stops: it keeps continuously rolling slowly forward, visibly receding and shrinking as it pulls away, so the gap widens. The truck is the single moving object in the frame.
segments.key_changes: Absolute halt of the ego (no scrolling of road, lane lines, or poles at all), contrasted with the truck still rolling slowly forward and receding into the distance, the gap to it slowly widening.
segments.camera: Ego camera completely locked and static — no forward motion and no scrolling whatsoever — while the distant truck continues to roll forward and recede.

temporal_caption: At 0:00 the dashcam view shows a wide-open road with a slow flatbed truck carrying stacked, switched-off traffic signal heads very far ahead near the horizon in the same lane, its wheels turning as it continuously rolls slowly forward; the ego vehicle approaches at moderate speed with a very large gap between them. By 0:02 the ego vehicle begins braking early and slowing down while still far behind the truck, all signal lamps on the cargo remaining completely unlit with no glare on any lens, and the truck still rolling slowly forward. From 0:02 to 0:05 the ego sheds all of its speed and comes to a complete stop by 0:05, the road and roadside poles and lane markings scrolling ever more slowly and then coming fully to rest, while the truck keeps creeping forward and slowly pulls farther away, a large gap of several car-lengths remaining. From 0:05 to 0:08 the ego vehicle is completely halted and motionless — the road surface, lane markings, utility poles, parked cars, trees, and buildings are all entirely frozen with no scrolling at all — yet the truck never stops: it keeps rolling slowly forward, visibly receding and shrinking as it pulls away into the distance, the gap slowly widening, and it is the only moving thing anywhere in the frame.
audio_description: Ambient road noise of a moving vehicle, faint tire hum and wind, the engine note lowering steadily as the car decelerates early over several seconds and settling to a quiet idle as the vehicle comes to a complete stop by 0:05; from 0:05 onward only a steady idle hum and the faint, continuous low rumble of the distant truck still rolling away remain, with no ego road or tire motion noise. No speech or music.
